title: github reports "Error: direct image runtime not supported"
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

podman version 0fd8242 (2020 May 2) reports "Error: direct image runtime not supported"

Works for version 8857ba2 (2020 April 15)

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.  Run podman

**Describe the results you received:**

All runs give erro


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
[joe@big-apple RPMS]$ podman info --debug                                       │··············································
Error: direct image runtime not supported                                       │··············································
[joe@big-apple RPMS]$  
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
This seems to be triggered by 

https://github.com/containers/libpod/commit/554c663b5ad2bd0c0fa4c58559ec2a9b19ed399f

This looks like you are running podman-remote not podman?  Is this the rpm installed podman?
Did you use something like CRC to install podman?
I'm a packager for mageia and this is what happens when generate the rpm.  When I generate the rpm with sources before mid-April, the works.  When I run the spec file against May sources, it doesn't.

Here is the spec file.  Could something have changed with the build 

[podman.spec.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/4590987/podman.spec.txt)

The specific commit that causes problems is 241326a9a8c20ad7f2bcf651416b836e7778e090

Maybe some interaction with build script?
Or is there some migration between v1 and v2?
Think I found it.  I'm running go build directly and so the bulld args changed between V1 and V2

Greate.  @joequant There is huge changes going on in Master branch.  So you need to realize this.  If you want to go with the released branch `v1.9`, that should be more stable.

We are just finishing up getting podman V2.0 to pass `rootful` tests.  Next we will get it to pass `rootless` tests, followed by more distributions, and then on to making sure podman-remote is passing all tests.
In short, master is really not stable right now, so use at your own risk. We'll start cutting release candidates for v2.0 once we feel master is stable again, but that's probably a few weeks off.
